enforced.mr_name: Toggle RNEXT between explicit RNAME and '=' alias
enforced.oracle: Parsing the toggled file produces identical alignment semantics as the original file, since '=' is a valid synonym for 'same as RNAME'

evidence.quote: This field is set as `*' when the information is unavailable, and set as `=' if RNEXT is identical RNAME.

enforced.mr_name: FLAG unmapped bit with mapped RNAME/POS must be rejected
enforced.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH on the malformed record; silent acceptance is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.

enforced.mr_name: CIGAR query-consuming length mismatch with SEQ must be rejected
enforced.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH when CIGAR query-consuming op sum does not equal SEQ length; silent acceptance is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: If not a `*', the length of the sequence must equal the sum of lengths of MIS=X operations in CIGAR.

enforced.mr_name: TLEN sign consistency violation for paired reads must be rejected
enforced.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH when paired-end TLEN values have same sign instead of opposite signs; silent acceptance is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: The TLEN field is positive for the leftmost segment of the template, negative for the rightmost, and the sign for any middle segment is undefined. If segments cover the same coordinates then the choice of which is leftmost and rightmost is arbitrary, but the two ends must still have differing signs.

enforced.oracle: For records with FLAG bit 0x4 set, MAPQ should be 255 (not available) per spec; normalizing to 255 and round-tripping should preserve content

evidence.quote: MAPQ: MAPping Quality. It equals $-10_10\mapping position is wrong\$, rounded to the nearest integer. A value 255 indicates that the mapping quality is not available.

enforced.mr_name: HD header VN version bump round-trip invariance
enforced.oracle: Toggling @HD VN between SAM 1.5 and 1.6 should not change the semantic content of the file; parsing and re-serializing should yield equivalent canonical output

evidence.quote: VN* & Format version. Accepted format: /94[0-9]+92.[0-9]+\$/.

enforced.mr_name: Mate flag stripping for unpaired reads round-trip invariance
enforced.oracle: When FLAG bit 0x1 (multiple segments) is unset, clearing mate-context bits 0x2, 0x8, 0x20, 0x40, 0x80 should not change the semantic content since those bits are only meaningful when 0x1 is set; parsing and re-serializing should yield equivalent canonical output

evidence.quote: If 0x1 is unset, no assumptions can be made about 0x2, 0x8, 0x20, 0x40 and 0x80.

quarantine.mr_name: Permuting optional tag order on alignment lines is invariant
quarantine.oracle: The set of TAG:TYPE:VALUE tuples on each alignment line is identical before and after the transform; only their order changes. Parsing, validation, and all downstream operations produce identical results.

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.
Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: Shuffling TAG:VALUE pairs within @HD header line is invariant
quarantine.oracle: The set of TAG:VALUE pairs on the @HD line is identical before and after the transform; only their order changes. Parsing and all downstream operations produce identical results.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: Shuffling TAG:VALUE pairs within @SQ header lines is invariant
quarantine.oracle: The set of TAG:VALUE pairs on each @SQ line is identical before and after the transform; only their order changes. Parsing and all downstream operations produce identical results.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: Shuffling TAG:VALUE pairs within @RG header lines is invariant
quarantine.oracle: The set of TAG:VALUE pairs on each @RG line is identical before and after the transform; only their order changes. Parsing and all downstream operations produce identical results.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: Shuffling TAG:VALUE pairs within @PG header lines is invariant
quarantine.oracle: The set of TAG:VALUE pairs on each @PG line is identical before and after the transform; only their order changes. Parsing and all downstream operations produce identical results.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: Shuffling @CO comment line order is invariant
quarantine.oracle: The set of @CO comment lines is identical before and after the transform; only their order changes. Parsing and all downstream operations produce identical results.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

ambiguity_flags: @CO lines are explicitly excluded from the TAG:VALUE format rule, and the spec treats them as free-text comments with no ordering semantics. The spec does not explicitly state that @CO line order is insignificant, but the regex pattern /94@CO92t.*/ and the fact that @CO lines lack structured TAG:VALUE pairs implies they are free-text comments whose order carries no semantics.

quarantine.oracle: The set of @SQ lines is identical before and after the transform; only their order changes. Parsing and all downstream operations produce identical results, provided no coordinate-sorted alignment depends on @SQ order.

evidence.quote: For coordinate sort, the major sort key is the RNAME field, with order defined by the order of @SQ lines in the header.

ambiguity_flags: @SQ order defines the sort order for coordinate-sorted files (SO:coordinate). Reordering @SQ lines changes the coordinate sort order semantics, so this MR is only valid when SO is not coordinate (SO:unsorted or SO:queryname) or when the file is not sorted by coordinate. The spec does not mandate any particular @SQ order for unsorted/queryname-sorted files.

quarantine.oracle: Parsing the uppercased file produces identical alignment data as the original file, since the spec states no assumptions can be made on letter cases

evidence.quote: No assumptions can be made on the letter cases.

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'No assumptions can be made on the letter cases' which is permissive but some parsers may treat upper/lower case differently for sequence comparison

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR split/merge invariance for adjacent identical operations
quarantine.oracle: After splitting or merging adjacent identical CIGAR operations, the total query-consumed length (sum of M,I,S,=,X lengths) and reference-consumed length (sum of M,D,N,=,X lengths) remain unchanged; the alignment semantics are preserved.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: [SAM v1 — Recommended Practice for the SAM Format] * Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: ADVISORY: 'should be different' is a recommendation, not a strict requirement — parsers may accept adjacent identical ops

quarantine.oracle: Removing zero-length CIGAR ops and merging newly-adjacent identical ops preserves the alignment's query-consumed length, reference-consumed length, and all other fields.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: [SAM v1 — Recommended Practice for the SAM Format] * Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: ADVISORY: The spec recommends but does not require adjacent ops to be different; zero-length ops are not explicitly forbidden

quarantine.oracle: Converting between hard-clipping (H) and soft-clipping (S) in CIGAR with synchronized SEQ/QUAL preserves the alignment's reference coordinates and mate-pair semantics.

evidence.quote: H can only be present as the first and/or last operation. S may only have H operations between them and the ends of the CIGAR string.

ambiguity_flags: The spec constrains placement of H and S but does not define equivalence between them; the transform assumes H⇌S equivalence for testing purposes

quarantine.mr_name: Optional tag with illegal type character must be rejected
quarantine.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH when an optional tag's type character is not one of AifZHB; silent acceptance is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: As with alignment optional fields (see Section~sec:alnaux), you can freely add new tags for further data fields.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly enumerate the valid type characters (AifZHB) in the chunk retrieved; the SAMtags §2.1 table defining these types was not returned below threshold. The rule is well-established in the SAM specification but the evidence chunk only references optional fields indirectly.

quarantine.mr_name: POS shift with matching LN extension preserves coordinate validity
quarantine.oracle: After shifting all mapped records' POS by +shift and increasing the corresponding @SQ LN by the same shift, the file must remain valid: for every mapped record, POS + sum_of_reference_consuming_CIGAR_ops (M, =, X, D, N) must be <= LN. The round-trip through the SUT parser/writer must produce identical alignment data (same POS, RNAME, CIGAR) as the transformed file.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p138
evidence.quote: * If POS plus the sum of lengths of M=XDN operations in CIGAR exceeds the length specified in the LN field of the @SQ header line (if exists) with an SN equal to RNAME, the alignment should be unmapped, unless the reference sequence is circular (see below).
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

evidence.quote: 4 &  POS & Int & $[0,\,2^31-1]$ & 1-based leftmost mapping POSition

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'should be unmapped' (ADVISORY), not 'must' — some parsers may accept overhanging alignments without error
